2606.13537.title: When Does Mixing Help? Analyzing Query Embedding Interpolation in Multilingual Dense Retrieval
2606.13537.paper_summary: While mixed-language querying is ubiquitous in multilingual communities, the sensitivity of dense retrievers to such queries remains poorly understood. We present a ratio-controlled study on mMARCO that systematically evaluates retrieval performance by varying the mixing proportion of parallel query translations via embedding-level mixing -- constructing mixed queries as an interpolation of monolingual embeddings. Experiments with BGE-M3 demonstrate that an optimal mixing ratio outperforms the best monolingual endpoint in 88/105 cases. We uncover a distinct asymmetry driven by English dominance: mixing is uniformly beneficial when retrieving from non-English document indices, whereas indices containing English are best served by pure English queries. Furthermore, English acts as the strongest mixing partner for every non-English document language. Finally, when controlling for English dominance, mixing gains correlate negatively with typological distance. We conclude that language-mix sensitivity is structured and predictable, and we validate the robustness of these patterns across model families and scales.
2606.13537.paper_summary_zh: 雖然混合語言查詢在多語言社區中無處不在，但密集檢索器對這類查詢的敏感性仍然不甚了解。<lb/>我們在 mMARCO 上進行了一項比率控制的研究，系統性地通過改變平行查詢翻譯的混合比例來評估檢索性能，這是通過嵌入級混合構建混合查詢，將單語嵌入進行插值。<lb/>使用 BGE-M3 的實驗表明，最佳混合比例在 88/105 的案例中超越了最佳單語端點。<lb/>我們揭示了一種由英語主導驅動的明顯不對稱性：在從非英語文檔索引中檢索時，混合是均勻有益的，而包含英語的索引則最適合使用純英語查詢。<lb/>此外，英語對每種非英語文檔語言來說都是最強的混合夥伴。<lb/>最後，在控制英語主導的情況下，混合增益與類型距離呈負相關。我們得出結論，語言混合敏感性是有結構且可預測的，並且我們驗證了這些模式在模型家族和規模上的穩健性。
2606.13537.author: Tongyao Zhu et.al.
2606.13537.authors: Tongyao Zhu, Chao-Ming Huang, Min-Yen Kan
